christensen.mechanism: Sequential plastic deformation of steel billets through custom-geometry roll sets (hot rolling) and carbide die sets (cold drawing/rolling) to produce near-net-shape 2D cross-sections, with optional selective induction surface hardening via high-frequency electromagnetic heating and rapid quench
christensen.function: Provide custom cross-sectional metal shapes with tight dimensional tolerances and selectable surface hardness in near-net-shape geometry
christensen.outcome: Enable OEM manufacturers and fabricators to eliminate 40-80% of downstream machining waste, reduce material costs, and shorten production lead times by starting from a near-final-shape input

technology.underlyingMechanism: Sequential plastic deformation of steel billets through custom-geometry roll sets (hot rolling) and carbide die sets (cold drawing/rolling), achieving near-net-shape 2D cross-sections with optional selective induction surface hardening via high-frequency electromagnetic heating and rapid quench. Key physics: plastic deformation below (cold) or above (hot) recrystallization temperature; martensitic surface transformation via rapid quench.
technology.unspscPath: 30 Structures and Building and Construction and Manufacturing Components and Supplies > 10 Structural components and basic shapes > 23 Profiles > 04 Steel profiles

functionalPromise.statement: Provide custom cross-sectional metal shapes with tight dimensional tolerances and selectable surface hardness — in near-net-shape geometry that eliminates 40-80% of downstream machining waste
functionalPromise.context: with tight dimensional tolerances and selectable surface hardness

commodityFunctionalPromise: Provide elongated metal shapes with defined cross-sectional geometry for structural, mechanical, or guiding functions
differentiators: Near-net-shape geometry eliminates 40-80% material removal vs. machining from bar stock
IT8 tolerance class (+-0.02 mm) achievable via cold finishing per ISO 286
Selective induction surface hardening up to 64 HRC with controllable depth (0.5-5 mm), ductile core retained
Full alloy library spanning carbon, alloy, bearing, spring, and tool steels through common forming equipment
In-line induction hardening integrated into production flow — no outsourcing to third-party heat treaters
IATF 16949 certified for direct automotive OEM supply — not all European profile competitors hold this certification

features.name: Custom Die-Set Forming
features.short: Form complex 2D cross-sections via purpose-built roller and die sets
features.long: Each profile geometry requires a dedicated die or roll set designed for the target cross-section. Hot rolling achieves bulk shape from billet; cold drawing/rolling adds dimensional precision. Die cost is amortized over production volume. Minimum order quantities apply per new geometry. Profile types include round, square, hex, rectangular, symmetric custom, and asymmetric custom.

features.name: Near-Net-Shape Geometry
features.short: Produce profiles requiring minimal downstream machining, eliminating 40-80% material removal vs. bar stock
features.long: Profile cross-section closely matches final part geometry, eliminating 40-80% of material removal versus machining from bar stock. Economic benefit scales with part complexity and production volume. Requires upfront die design investment per geometry.

features.name: Precision Cold Finishing
features.short: Achieve IT8-class tolerances (+-0.02 mm) and Ra 0.4 um surface via cold drawing or cold rolling
features.long: Room-temperature drawing through carbide dies or rolling through precision rolls refines dimensions to IT8 class (+-0.02 mm) per ISO 286. Work-hardening increases tensile strength (up to 1,200 MPa for 34CrNiMo6). Surface roughness improves to Ra 0.4 um (category N8 per DIN-ISO 1302). Limited to cross-sections within cold-forming equipment capacity (2,500 mm2 for drawing).

features.name: Selective Induction Surface Hardening
features.short: Harden specific surface zones up to 64 HRC via electromagnetic induction heating and quench
features.long: High-frequency induction coils heat targeted surface areas above austenitizing temperature, followed by rapid water quench. Produces hard wear-resistant surface (up to 64 HRC) while core remains ductile. Hardening depth controllable from 0.5-5.0 mm. Requires minimum 0.35% carbon content for martensite formation. Geometry-dependent: hardening pattern must match profile shape.

features.name: Multi-Alloy Grade Processing
features.short: Process carbon, alloy, bearing, spring, and tool steels through common forming equipment
features.long: Rolling and drawing equipment accommodates diverse steel grades by adjusting process parameters (temperature, reduction ratio, speed, die design, lubrication). Each grade requires grade-specific heat treatment cycles (normalizing, Q&T, case hardening). Material qualification for new grades typically takes 3-6 months.

features.name: Continuous-Length Production
features.short: Deliver profiles as bars (up to 12 m) or coils (up to 2,000 kg)
features.long: Hot-rolled profiles delivered as bars up to 12 m or coils up to 2,000 kg. Cold-drawn profiles as bars up to 6 m. Length tolerance +-2 mm. Straightness 0.2 mm/m or better. Enables downstream cutting to customer length specifications.

features.name: Proprietary Die Library
features.short: Access 300+ years of accumulated die designs and metallurgical knowledge across 12+ sectors
features.long: ZOLLERN's die library, accumulated since 1708, contains proven geometries across 12+ application sectors (linear guides, firearms, elevator rails, etc.). Reduces new-geometry development risk and lead time for profiles similar to existing designs. Not transferable to competitors.

features.name: In-Line Induction Hardening Integration
features.short: Perform hardening within profile production flow (up to 4,000 mm2, lengths 2,200-9,000 mm)
features.long: ZOLLERN's induction hardening is integrated into the production line rather than outsourced to third-party heat treaters. Machine handles cross-sections up to 4,000 mm2 and part lengths from 2,200 to 9,000 mm. Reduces lead time and transport cost versus external hardening.

features.name: IATF 16949 Certified Supply Chain
features.short: Supply automotive OEMs under automotive quality management certification
features.long: ZOLLERN holds IATF 16949 certification for the Steel Profiles division, enabling direct supply to automotive OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers. Requires ongoing audit compliance, PPAP documentation, and SPC monitoring. Not all European profile competitors hold this certification.

constraints.name: 2D Constant Cross-Section Only
constraints.description: Cannot produce 3D-varying geometry along length; profile cross-section is constant end to end. Variable cross-section applications (tapered shafts, sculptured surfaces) cannot be served.

constraints.name: Max Cold-Draw Cross-Section
constraints.description: Cold drawing limited by draw bench capacity and force requirements.

constraints.name: Max Profile Width
constraints.description: Rolling and drawing equipment limits maximum circumscribed width of profile cross-section.

constraints.name: Max Induction Hardening Cross-Section
constraints.description: Induction hardening machine capacity limits maximum hardenable cross-section.

constraints.name: Min Carbon Content for Hardening
constraints.description: Induction hardening requires minimum carbon for martensite formation. Low-carbon structural steels (S235, S355) and austenitic stainless steels cannot be hardened.

constraints.name: Steel-Only Material Scope
constraints.description: Steel and stainless steel only. No aluminum, titanium, copper, or non-ferrous metals. Die and roll designs are specific to steel deformation characteristics.

constraints.name: Min Economic Batch for Custom Die
constraints.description: Custom die/roll design, manufacture, and qualification costs must be amortized over minimum volume. Sub-100 kg orders for new geometries are not economically viable.

constraints.name: Custom Tooling Lead Time
constraints.description: New die/roll design, manufacture, trial runs, and dimensional qualification. Repeat orders for existing geometries have no tooling lead time.

constraints.name: Induction Hardening Length Range
constraints.description: Induction hardening machine accepts specified part lengths within this range only.

billOfMaterials.function: Raw feedstock for hot rolling — carbon, alloy, bearing, spring, or tool steel grades
billOfMaterials.keyAttribute: Grade determines final mechanical properties; C45, C60, 42CrMo4, 16MnCr5, 34CrNiMo6, 100Cr6

billOfMaterials.function: Form bulk cross-section geometry from billet via hot plastic deformation
billOfMaterials.keyAttribute: Geometry-specific; amortized over production volume; ZOLLERN die library since 1708

billOfMaterials.function: Refine dimensions to IT8 tolerance (+-0.02 mm) via room-temperature drawing
billOfMaterials.keyAttribute: Tungsten carbide dies; produces Ra 0.4 um surface finish

billOfMaterials.function: Achieve target metallurgical properties — grain structure, hardness, ductility balance
billOfMaterials.keyAttribute: Grade-specific cycles; 3-6 month qualification for new grades

billOfMaterials.function: Selectively harden surface zones up to 64 HRC via electromagnetic induction and quench
billOfMaterials.keyAttribute: In-line integrated; cross-sections up to 4,000 mm2; lengths 2,200-9,000 mm

billOfMaterials.function: Cutting, drilling, milling, grinding to customer part specifications
billOfMaterials.keyAttribute: Optional value-add step; converts semi-finished profile to near-finished component
